notes: Decider run 2026-04-06T22:28. 7 patches: 2 critical WIN-005 fixes (re-issued from 2026-04-06T23-00 — still unapplied), ISS-465 SEC-5.15 Schumann direction fix (EXP-009 patch for ISS-465), and 4 EXP-007 sections.json text patches (changes 6-9 of 9; changes 1-5 require manual generate-html.js edits — see ISS-489 in open-issues for instructions).

patches.rationale: CRITICAL — re-issued (originally in 2026-04-06T23-00.json, still unapplied). WIN-005 CHAOS-7 DOI fix: current DOI (10.1186/s40623-020-01163-9) links to a different paper in the same journal. Correct DOI is 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 (Olsen et al. 2020 CHAOS-7 model). Citation must be verifiable for the review to be credible. Identified by curmudgeon Cycle 1 and confirmed in Cycle 2.

CRITICAL — re-issued (originally in 2026-04-06T23-00.json, still unapplied). WIN-005 monitoring tag corrected from 'live_fetch' to 'hardcoded'. Dome's monitor.py does NOT dynamically fetch CHAOS-7 model outputs — it hardcodes observed values (23,050 to 21,880 nT) in inject_ai_layer.py and threshold-checks a single TTB station. 'live_fetch' misrepresents dome infrastructure.

patches.find: The Schumann resonance is computed using a uniform cavity height (f = c/4H₀). No script in the repository averages H(r) over the dome's exponential profile. The "simplified" version on the website is the only version that exists.
patches.replace: The Schumann frequency is an INPUT, not an output. The Schumann frequency 7.83 Hz appears only as an INPUT to the dome's calculations (to derive cavity height H = c/4f), never as an OUTPUT of dome geometry. No script in the repository takes dome parameters as input and produces 7.83 Hz as a prediction — because doing so with the exponential profile would yield ~22 Hz (see Section 4.5.1). Using the correct exponential H(r) in the resonance formula yields f ≈ 22 Hz — nearly three times the observed 7.83 Hz. The dome uses the observed frequency to calibrate H₀, then treats that calibrated constant as a geometric prediction.
patches.rationale: MAJOR: SEC-5.15 Schumann direction fix. Current text says the resonance 'is computed using uniform cavity height' implying 7.83 Hz is an output. Corrected: 7.83 Hz is the INPUT used to calibrate H₀ = c/4f. Using the actual exponential H(r) yields ~22 Hz (the self-contradiction shown in Section 4.5.1). This is calibration masquerading as prediction. Patch derived from EXP-009 analyst output (rationale: 'Addresses ISS-465'). Verified find string matches current sections.json.

patches.find: Part 4.5: Internal Contradictions — Does the Dome's Geometry Produce Its Claimed Predictions?
patches.replace: Part 4.5: Self-Contradictions — The Dome's Own Geometry Refutes Its Claims
patches.rationale: EXP-007 change 9 (sections.json portion): Update Part 4.5 heading to use 'Self-Contradictions' label. Clearer, more direct, consistent with tab reorder intent. Note: Full EXP-007 tab reorder also requires 5 manual changes to generate-html.js (tab button order, sectionNav links, TOC entries) — see ISS-489 for instructions. This patch handles the text changes that CAN be applied via sections.json.

patches.find: The most damaging critique of the dome model is not external data, but the model's own internal geometry. When the author's stated equations are applied honestly — without substitution of globe formulas — they produce predictions that contradict both observations and the author's claims. Below are twelve cases where the dome geometry refutes itself.
patches.replace:
Why these arguments come first: The strongest test of any scientific model is internal consistency — do its own equations produce its own claimed predictions? External data can always be disputed ("your instruments assume the globe"), but when a model's own stated parameters contradict its own claims, there is no frame of reference to hide behind. Both sides of every argument below use only dome-stated values: H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619), disc radius = 20,015 km, sun altitude = 5,733 km, moon altitude = 2,534 km. If the dome's own geometry predicts the wrong answer, the model refutes itself — no satellite data, no globe assumptions, no external measurements required.

When the author's stated equations are applied honestly — without substitution of globe formulas — they produce predictions that contradict both observations and the author's claims. Below are {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} cases where the dome geometry refutes itself. The External Falsification Tests that follow provide independent confirmation from satellite data, astrometry, and direct measurement — but the verdict is already determined here, from the dome's own math.
patches.rationale: EXP-007 change 6 (sections.json portion): Add framing paragraph to Part 4.5 opening explaining why self-contradictions come first — they use only dome-stated values, so no 'globe assumption' objection can apply. Also fixes hardcoded 'twelve cases' to use {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} placeholder (computed at build time). Uses EXP-007 replacement_opening text adapted for sections.json ({{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} replaces ${counts.selfContradicted}).

EXP-007 change 8 (sections.json portion): Update Part 4 heading to reflect its new role as the second argument tab (external data confirming what self-contradictions already established). 'External' clarifies the distinction from Part 4.5's internal geometry arguments.

patches.find: If the model is a genuine physical model, it must make predictions that differ from the globe model. Below are concrete, repeatable measurements that are incompatible with an elliptical flat disc topped by a copper dome.
patches.replace: The self-contradiction analysis above shows the dome model refutes itself using only its own stated parameters. The tests below provide independent confirmation from external data sources — satellite measurements, space telescopes, seismology, and direct observation. These are not needed to establish the verdict (the dome's own math already fails), but they confirm the failure from multiple independent directions. A dome defender may object that external measurements "assume the globe" — but the measurements below include direct observations (solar angular diameter, flight distances) that require no theoretical framework at all.
patches.rationale: EXP-007 change 7 (sections.json portion): Add bridge paragraph to Part 4 opening acknowledging the self-contradictions already established in Part 4.5, and framing external tests as confirmatory rather than primary. Preempts 'globe assumption' objection by noting some tests require no theoretical framework. From EXP-007 replacement_opening for falsify tab.
